findings.message: CLAUDE.md Repository Structure section (lines 7-25) and install examples (lines 228-234) describe three separate top-level plugin directories (expo-app-design/, upgrading-expo/, expo-cicd-workflows/) that do not exist; all skills are consolidated under plugins/expo/ as a single plugin.

Instruction 'its first child should almost always be a ScrollView' uses the vague quantifier 'almost always' without stating the exceptions; actionable guidance should be unconditional or enumerate the exceptions explicitly.
Instruction 'it should almost always be the first component inside the route component' uses the vague quantifier 'almost always'.
Instruction 'Whenever possible, include a <Link.Preview>' is vague — no criterion for when it is impossible or undesirable.
Instruction 'Use link previews frequently to enhance navigation' is vague — 'frequently' gives no actionable criterion.
Rule 'Keep API routes focused — one responsibility per endpoint' — 'focused' is vague; the clarification 'one responsibility' partially rescues it but 'focused' adds no information.
Rule 'Handle errors gracefully with try/catch' — 'gracefully' is a vague adverb with no actionable meaning in this context.

Commands reference '{baseDir}' (lines 19, 82, 84) with no explanation of whether Claude Code auto-substitutes this value or the agent must resolve it manually; a reader unfamiliar with the plugin system will not know how to run these commands.

Script accepts any URL via process.argv[2] and makes an HTTP fetch request without validating the domain or scheme; an attacker supplying a crafted URL (e.g. http://169.254.169.254/) could trigger SSRF if the script is invoked outside the documented skill context.
Dependency 'ajv' specified as '^8.17.1' allows automatic minor and patch upgrades; without a committed lockfile this could pull in a supply-chain-compromised version.
Dependency 'ajv-formats' specified as '^3.0.1' allows automatic upgrades without a pinned lockfile.
Dependency 'js-yaml' specified as '^4.1.0' allows automatic upgrades without a pinned lockfile.

In-body reference links use './reference/' (singular) at lines 126, 127, 130, 133, 165 but the top-level References section at lines 18-22 uses './references/' (plural); the singular-form links point to a non-existent directory.
Frontmatter 'name' field is 'Expo UI Jetpack Compose' — must be lowercase kebab-case per plugin conventions (max 64 chars, lowercase, hyphens only).

Code example imports from '@expo-ui/swift-ui' but frontmatter description and install instructions specify '@expo/ui/swift-ui'; these are different packages and the example import will fail at runtime.
Frontmatter 'name' field is 'Expo UI SwiftUI' — must be lowercase kebab-case per plugin conventions.
Decision tree branch label 'Complex app -> React Query' uses 'complex' without defining the threshold; a developer cannot self-assess whether their app qualifies.

Code example imports from '@expo-ui/swift-ui' but frontmatter description and install instructions specify '@expo/ui/swift-ui'; these are different packages and the example import will fail at runtime.

In-body reference links use './reference/' (singular) at lines 126, 127, 130, 133, 165 but the top-level References section at lines 18-22 uses './references/' (plural); the singular-form links point to a non-existent directory.

Script accepts any URL via process.argv[2] and makes an HTTP fetch request without validating the domain or scheme; an attacker supplying a crafted URL (e.g. http://169.254.169.254/) could trigger SSRF if the script is invoked outside the documented skill context.

Dependency 'ajv-formats' specified as '^3.0.1' allows automatic upgrades without a pinned lockfile.
Dependency 'js-yaml' specified as '^4.1.0' allows automatic upgrades without a pinned lockfile.
Frontmatter 'name' field is 'Expo UI Jetpack Compose' — must be lowercase kebab-case per plugin conventions (max 64 chars, lowercase, hyphens only).
Frontmatter 'name' field is 'Expo UI SwiftUI' — must be lowercase kebab-case per plugin conventions.

Instruction 'its first child should almost always be a ScrollView' uses the vague quantifier 'almost always' without stating the exceptions; actionable guidance should be unconditional or enumerate the exceptions explicitly.

Instruction 'Whenever possible, include a <Link.Preview>' is vague — no criterion for when it is impossible or undesirable.
Instruction 'Use link previews frequently to enhance navigation' is vague — 'frequently' gives no actionable criterion.
